author: Shahab-ud-din, Qazi
title: Qissa-i-Dahud Badshah
gloss: For subject see entry No. 190, at page 16 of the Catalogue for the quarter ending the 31st December, 1909.
notes: Dual date: 1327 Hijri / October 1910. Cross-reference 1909Q4 entry 190, page 16.

title: Temperance Git, Hissa-i-Siwum
gloss: Verses directed against the use of intoxicating drinks.
notes: Persian-script part 3 of the Temperance Git series; part 4 in Gurmukhi at p11 s263 same quarter — temperance campaign running in both scripts.

title: Siri Guru Granth Sahib ji Wichon Chalane de Shabad jo Khalsa Dharam Vikhe Mritak Sanskar Samen gaven jande han
gloss: Verses from the Sikh Granth sung at Sikh funerals.
notes: Serial continues from Q3 Punjabi—Religion (ended s13). Funerary liturgy codified in tract form — Khalsa Tract Society standardizing ritual (mritak sanskar) practice.

title: Shudhi arthat Khalsa Patat Pawan
gloss: A dialogue between a Sikh in favour of converting men of other religious persuasion to Sikhism and another against it. The former justifies his assertions with quotations from the Hindu scriptures and the Sikh Grantha.
notes: 'Shudhi, that is, the Khalsa purifier of the fallen' — Sikh appropriation of the Arya Samaj shuddhi (reconversion) debate in dialogue form; core homogenization-dynamics document.

author: Nihal Singh, Mistri
title: Pustak Silp Vidiya Prakash, Bhag I
gloss: A treatise on designing and architecture.
publisher: Mistri Batan Singh, Dosanjh Khurd, district Jullundur
copyright: B. Batan Singh, Dosanjh Khurd, district Jullundur; No. 18—, dated the — December 1910.
notes: Artisan (mistri) authorship of a Punjabi architecture treatise, copyright-registered — vernacular technical knowledge asserting property rights. Serial continues from Q3 (s3).

flags.issue: copyright cell partially cut at page edge; No. and date digits illegible (No. 18—, dated 3?th December 1910)

author: Ghulam Nabi, Hakim, Dr.
title: Zubdat-ut-Tajarib
gloss: A pharmacopœia in Sindhi, with a description of diseases.
notes: First SINDHI section of 1910 in our extractions — Sindhi pharmacopoeia printed at Lahore, distributed free. Author styled both Hakim and Dr. Date printed 'Octobe , 1910'.
